note: Templates for the semantic_resolvable conflict category (ANALYSIS_PLAN_ADDENDUM.md item A2): equal source, equal confidence, uninformative timestamp on both branches. Ground truth is recoverable only by checking which branch value is semantically compatible with the ancestor's stated constraint at the SAME (entity, predicate) key -- not from any structured metadata field. IMPORTANT: ancestor_predicate must equal predicate, since ThreeWayLLMMerge's prompt only includes the ancestor's value for the exact colliding key (see branchmem/merge/three_way_llm.py); a constraint stored under a different predicate is invisible to the model regardless of its reasoning ability (caught via a real diagnostic check on the first run of this experiment, PROGRESS.md).

templates.ancestor_value: has a peanut allergy and avoids peanut-based food
uses a wheelchair and requires ramp access, no stairs
wants a quiet hotel, away from nightlife noise
templates.branch_b_value: hotel is in a quiet residential area

templates.ancestor_value: cannot drink alcohol for health reasons
gets cold easily and dislikes air conditioning
templates.branch_a_value: room has AC set to maximum

templates.ancestor_value: gets severe motion sickness on boats
templates.branch_b_value: itinerary uses the land bridge instead

templates.ancestor_value: is only available before noon local time
has a knee injury and avoids high-impact exercise
